definitions.gloss_en: the playing surface representing the battlefield; the zone of play
definitions.gloss_jp: {ゲーム|げーむ}の{プレイ|ぷれい}{エリア|えりあ}全{体|たい}；{対|たい}{戦|せん}が{行|おこな}われる{空|くう}{間|かん}
definitions.explanation_en: The entire play area (table surface or defined zone) on which units are deployed and combat occurs.
definitions.implicit_notes: 戦場 frames the entire game area as a single field of battle. It is a classical Japanese literary and military term, carrying historical and dramatic weight.
definitions.orthography_notes: 戦場: 戦 (battle) + 場 (place/field). Classical Japanese military and literary term. Attested in Goo dictionary as a general word. JWC documentation uses 戦場 consistently. No WH-specific concern.
definitions.game_setting_notes: Universal in wargame contexts. The docx notes this term is already in use and has classical Japanese military precedent with no Warhammer connection.

usage_jp: {戦|せん}{場|じょう}に{ユニット|ゆにっと}を{配|はい}{置|ち}する (deploy units on the battlefield); {戦|せん}{場|じょう}の{中|ちゅう}{央|おう} (center of the battlefield)
comparative_notes: 戦場 is the standard term for the playing surface in Japanese wargame documentation (JWC). No alternative is required.

examples.japanese: {戦|せん}{場|じょう}の{中|ちゅう}{央|おう}を{制|せい}{圧|あつ}する。
examples.english: Control the center of the battlefield.

cross_references.note: 位置 — a unit's location within the 戦場
地形 — terrain that defines the 戦場's features
